entry.title: SAP CPI to Microsoft SharePoint Online – Certificate-Based OAuth2 Client Credentials
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are currently implementing an integration between SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) and Microsoft SharePoint Online.

The SharePoint team has confirmed the following requirements:

  • Direct Client Certificate Authentication (mTLS) is not supported.
  • Authentication must be performed via Microsoft Entra ID App Registration.
  • Client Secret authentication is not preferred due to password expiry and security requirements.
  • Certificate-Based OAuth2 Client Credentials authentication is the recommended approach.

While evaluating the standard authentication options available in SAP CPI, we could not identify an out-of-the-box configuration for OAuth2 Client Credentials using certificate-based authentication (Private Key JWT / JWT Assertion) by leveraging an existing CPI Key Pair (certificate and private key).

The options currently available in our CPI tenant are:

  • OAuth2 Client Credentials
  • OAuth2 Authorization Code
  • OAuth2 SAML Bearer Assertion
  • Client Certificate Authentication

However, we could not find a standard way to configure OAuth2 Client Credentials with certificate/private key mapping for Microsoft Entra ID without implementing custom JWT generation logic through Groovy scripting.

I would appreciate feedback from anyone who has worked on a similar scenario.

Questions:

  1. Has anyone successfully implemented SAP CPI to Microsoft SharePoint Online integration using Microsoft Entra ID App Registration and certificate-based OAuth2 Client Credentials authentication?

  2. Were you able to achieve this using only standard CPI capabilities?

  3. If custom development was required, did you implement JWT generation through Groovy scripting or use an alternative approach?

  4. Has SAP provided any standard capability, roadmap item, SAP Note, or recommended approach for this authentication pattern?

  5. What is the generally accepted best practice for SAP CPI integrations where Microsoft Entra ID requires certificate-based authentication instead of client secret authentication?

Any guidance, implementation experience, SAP Notes, blogs, or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration

entry.title: Re: Workflows for Background Portlet
entry.content: Can anyone explain how the text is being populated by default? I tried using the same report, but the Awards text is not being populated automatically in my case. Also, we cannot grant users permission to manually enter data into this field. Could you please let me know how this field is being populated by default?

entry.title: ME52Nで品目変更時にEBAN-LFDAT（納入日付）が自動変更される事象について
entry.content:
現象】
購買依頼(PR)をME51Nで新規登録した後、ME52Nで品目（材料/品目コード等）のみを変更すると、当該品目行の納入日付(EBAN-LFDAT)が意図せず変わってしまう。
品目以外の項目（数量、納入日付自体など）は変更していないにもかかわらず、納入日付が自動的に再計算・更新される。

【質問したい内容】

  1. 品目変更時にEBAN-LFDAT（納入日付）が自動更新されるのはS/4HANA標準仕様か。
  2. 標準仕様の場合、納入日付は何を基準に再計算されるのか（例:品目マスタの計画引渡期間/Planned Delivery Time、購買情報レコード、発注日付からの日数計算等、再計算ロジックの根拠を確認したい）。

entry.title: Re: Ongoing Lessons Learnt integrating SmartRecruiters to SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content:
Chris

I think it really depends on the size and complexity of your customer.  Personally, I would run all the design workshop for all your processes so you know what you are dealing with.  Once the integrations evolve, I would look at deploying certain processes.

Re pay info, this need to be one the position to send it to SR, not much you can do about it at the moment.

entry.title: How to Configure SEPA Instant Transfers in SAP (DMEE / CGI Format)
entry.content:
How to Configure SEPA Instant Transfers in SAP (DMEE / CGI Format)

Tags: Payroll FI-AP DMEE SEPA BAdI CGI

The Problem

A recurring question in SAP communities and support channels: how do you make SAP generate <Cd>INST</Cd> in the payment XML so the bank processes the payment as an instant transfer?

Many customers configure the payment method in transaction FBZP and find no option to mark a transfer as "instant payment." After searching through notes and community threads, the instructions seem scattered and contradictory. This post consolidates the answer.

What needs to happen in the XML

For the bank to process a transfer as a SEPA Instant Credit Transfer (SCT Inst), the generated XML file (pain.001 format) must carry the INST indicator. The standard requires adding a <SvcLvl> (Service Level) block inside the payment information (<PmtInf>) or within each individual credit transfer (<CdtTrfTxInf>). This value tells the bank to route the order through the SCT Inst scheme rather than the standard SEPA Credit Transfer.

Option A — Service Level at payment batch level

<PmtInf>
  <PmtTpInf>
    <SvcLvl>
      <Cd>SEPA</Cd>
    </SvcLvl>
    <LclInstrm>
      <Cd>INST</Cd>
    </LclInstrm>
  </PmtTpInf>
  <!-- ... rest of payment info ... -->
</PmtInf>

Option B — Local Instrument at transaction level

<CdtTrfTxInf>
  <PmtTpInf>
    <LclInstrm>
      <Cd>INST</Cd>
    </LclInstrm>
  </PmtTpInf>
  <!-- ... rest of transaction ... -->
</CdtTrfTxInf>

The <LclInstrm><Cd>INST</Cd> node is the key signal. Banks supporting SCT Inst will detect it and apply the instant processing rules (funds available within 10 seconds, 24/7/365).

This node appears at two levels in the pain.001 structure: - Batch level (<PmtInf><PmtTpInf>) — for SEPA payments - Transaction level (<PmtInf><CdtTrfTxInf><PmtTpInf>) — for non-SEPA payments

Why it doesn't work out of the box

The CGI DMEE trees control this node through the exit function FI_CGI_DMEE_EXIT_W_BADI. If you look at the standard implementation, you'll find this code:

WHEN '<PmtInf><PmtTpInf><LclInstrm><Cd>'.
*   This node defines the Local instrument for the payment
    CLEAR c_value.

WHEN '<PmtInf><CdtTrfTxInf><PmtTpInf><LclInstrm><Cd>'.
*   This node defines the Local instrument for the payment
    CLEAR c_value.

SAP explicitly clears this node in standard for countries including ES and DE. There is no customizing switch to change this behavior — it requires a BAdI implementation.

Solution: BAdI FI_CGI_DMEE_COUNTRIES_CUST

The exit function calls two BAdIs in sequence:

BAdI Who can implement Purpose

FI_CGI_DMEE_COUNTRIESSAP onlyStandard country-specific logic
FI_CGI_DMEE_COUNTRIES_CUSTCustomerOverride values for specific nodes

The recommended approach is to keep the standard implementation intact and use the customer BAdI to override only the nodes that need modification.

Step-by-step

  1. Apply the latest CGI tree notes Implement all notes listed in the overview of SAP Note 2253571 (collective information note for CGI_XML_CT and CGI_XML_DD). The DMEE tree references exit function modules delivered by these notes — having an outdated implementation causes unexpected behavior.

  2. Create a BAdI implementation In transaction SE19 (or via SPRO), create an implementation for BAdI FI_CGI_DMEE_COUNTRIES_CUST in Enhancement Spot ES_IDFI_CGI_DMEE.

  3. Implement the method In the BAdI method, check the node path and assign the value INST when appropriate:

abap " Pseudocode — adapt to your business logic WHEN '<PmtInf><PmtTpInf><LclInstrm><Cd>'. c_value = 'INST'.

You can add conditions here to apply INST only to specific payment methods, house banks, or company codes — giving you full control over which payments are sent as instant transfers.

  1. Follow the official guide SAP Note 2253571 includes an attachment: 'Customer Enhancement in CGI with BAdI_150416.docx'. This document provides a full worked example of how to implement the customer BAdI correctly.

Alternative: PMW user exits (OBPM3 / OBPM1)

If the information needed by the bank is not available in the standard payment structures (FPAYH, FPAYP, FAPYHX), you can use Payment Medium Workbench exits to enrich the data before it reaches DMEE:

  • Event 05 (transaction OBPM1) → populates FPAYH-REFxx fields
  • Event 06 (transaction OBPM3) → populates FPAYHX-ZREFxx fields via a custom function module with the same interface as FI_PAYMEDIUM_SAMPLE_06. The ZREF fields are then mapped in the DMEEX tree.

Note: ZREF fields cannot be populated via Event 05 — use Event 06 for those.

Reference: SAP Note 965723 — PMW: use of exits (transaction OBPM3)

ECC vs S/4HANA / SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll

System CGI Tree Notes

ECC on-premiseCGI_XML_CT, CGI_XML_DDNo country-specific trees. Use BAdI CUST for all country/bank requirements.
S/4HANA on-premiseES_CGI_XML_CT (country-specific)Also available via note 2518834 / 2682253
SFECP (cloud)CGI_CT, CGI_DD (DMEEX engine)See note 2784858 and 3652001 for latest updates

Reference Notes

Note Content

2253571Collective note: CGI_XML_CT, CGI_XML_DD + BAdI enhancement guide
2241311CGI country-specific functionality for ES, IT (CBI), AT, DK, CN, TW, AU, HK, US, GB, MX, SE
3652001CGI: CGI_XML_CT, CGI_XML_DD Format Updates #49
2784858Collective note: CGI_CT, CGI_DD based on DMEEX engine
2518834Payment Formats available in S/4HANA 1709 on-premise
2682253Payment Formats supported in S/4HANA 1809 on-premise
965723PMW: use of exits (transaction OBPM3)

Summary

There is no standard customizing option to activate SEPA Instant Transfers in SAP payment files. The path is:

  1. Ensure CGI tree notes are up to date (note 2253571 overview)
  2. Implement BAdI FI_CGI_DMEE_COUNTRIES_CUST to set c_value = 'INST' for the <LclInstrm><Cd> node
  3. Add business logic conditions as needed (payment method, company code, etc.)
  4. Consult the example document in note 2253571 for implementation details

This is a consulting activity, not a software error — SAP does not plan to deliver INST as a standard default for any country. The BAdI is specifically provided for this kind of bank- and country-specific customization.

Have you implemented SEPA Instant Transfers in your system? Share your experience in the comments.

entry.title: to enable the mass upload functionality embedded in transaction KP26.
entry.content:
to enable the mass upload functionality embedded in transaction KP26. This goes in within transaction KP26 in settings > EXTRA > EXCEL PLANNING > UPLOAD.

Could you please advise standard configuration and upload file for embedded mass upload functionality in transaction KP26.

entry.title: Re: Can be maintain the OBYC table company code wise
entry.content:
If valuation is at company code level, then OBYC settings are effectively company code–specific. If valuation is at plant level, then OBYC entries are maintained per plant. OBYC is not a single table you edit directly. It is a customizing transaction that links transaction keys (e.g., BSX, GBB, WRX) with GL accounts. The determination depends on chart of accounts, valuation area, and sometimes account modifier.
Having said that, if multiple company codes share the same chart of accounts, then OBYC entries will be common across them. In that case, differentiation is only possible via valuation grouping codes or account modifiers.

entry.title: Credit Horizon Days restriction in Credit exposure Calculation
entry.content:
Hi,

The business requires the credit horizon to be restricted to 365 days for the credit exposure calculation. The exposure should include only values falling within the next 365 days for the following commitment categories:

  • Open Sales Contracts
  • Open Sales Orders
  • Open Receivables (uncleared).

We require your guidance regarding the credit horizon functionality in SAP FSCM Credit Management.

Regards,

Sandeep

entry.title: From Intent to Action with Joule Work & Knowledge Graph
entry.content:
Enterprise interaction is shifting from clicks to intent. Joule Work enables users to express what they want, while AI agents orchestrate processes, data, and applications behind the scenes. Powered by the Knowledge Graph, every action is grounded in business context and trusted data.

Join this session to explore how Joule Work works, how the Knowledge Graph enables meaningful AI, and what architects need to design—APIs, events, and semantic models—to make intent-driven enterprise a reality.

Register for other SAP Business AI Platform related events: https://events.sap.com/data-analytics/en_us/home.html

Live on YouTube: https://youtube.com/live/mr0U48Ay_ys?feature=share

entry.title: Re: Next-generation Just-In-Time Supply to Production | Part 2
entry.content:
Hi Simone/Marieo,

Could you please share the document related to the Next Generation JIT supply to production process? I am facing an issue while creating a JIT call manually through the Request Replenishment for Control Cycles Fiori app. I created a JIT call for the category Summarized JIT Call – Next Generation for a raw material with external replenishment. I have already maintained the prerequisites such as the source list, scheduling agreement with JIT relevance, and PSA, but I am still getting the error shown below.

Error :- Action 'Creation of JIT calls' not applicable for internal status.

Thanks and Regards

Shekhar

entry.title: Re: SAP BO to SAC mihgration
entry.content: anyone used NDC Converter 2.0 , need to know how it works so that such a third party can be created for business use

entry.title: Eligibility rule workaround in Non-EC instance
entry.content:
Hello,

I need the expert level advice regarding the compensation eligibility case. 

It is non-EC integrated environment. The customer has the complex (depended on multiple parametres) logic of who should be or not be included (eligible) in compensation worksheets. I can not provide all those parameters (data) via UDF file based on which I would then be able to create elibibility rule(s).
As per implementation Guide I can use one of the custom fields in UDF file and create eligibility rule based on it. But I wish not to use custom UDF fields for eligibility purposes.

I'm thinking to solve this issue this way:

1. set " Create worksheets by employee group" to true in comp plan parameters.

2. create static employee groups
3. include only eligible employees within the static groups

4. launch comp forms by groups

Now, I need to know if this workaround effectively achieves the same outcome as using a system rule to filter out ineligible employees?

What are the disadvantages or exceptions that I should consider before I offer this scenario to the customer?

Can anyone offer any alternate solution?

thanks

entry.title: Down payment scenario in France - Final Invoice lower than Down Payment Request
entry.content:
Hello,

We implemented the sales down payment process within scope item 7S7 (Sell from Stock with Down Payment Processing).

In our client’s business, it is common that the down payment is theoretically issued for 100% of the sales value, but the final price may change depending on the actual goods issue and % of active ingredient in final delivery. As a result, we encounter situations where, for example, the down payment is 36 EUR, while the final invoice amounts 24 EUR gross.

Unfortunately, SAP treats both documents independently. For instance, we have the following postings:

  • Down payment – we issue down payment request with gross amount 36 EUR,

     

  • We post incoming payment which calculates and generates VAT posting - 6 EUR according to standard 20% rate of VAT in France

     

  • Final invoice with final price after recalculation – posted as an additional document at final price.

     

This is incorrect, because the system effectively reports VAT on 6 EUR instead of 4,8 EUR (final price that should be used as a basis for VAT is 20 EUR gross).  Customer open items amount is also incorrect. It behaves as if two separate invoices were issued to the customer, whereas in reality the second document should function more like an adjustment, correcting the original 36 EUR down payment to the final amount of 24 EUR.

 

Could you please indicate whether there is any way to process this scenario so that VAT is calculated correctly, similarly to the customer open item?

 
Thank you in advance! 
KR
Mikolaj

entry.title: Re: GET Parameter ID in Update-Function-Module
entry.content: You can't GET that parameter in a background session. Either store it in a custom table & SESLECT form it or EXPORT to database in the  report and IMPORT form database in the batch job.

entry.title: Build Partner Products on SAP BTP ABAP Environment and Bring Them to SAP Store
entry.content:
Do you want to offer your solution to SAP customers as a cloud product?

SAP BTP ABAP Environment enables partners to build cloud products and side-by-side extensions with ABAP Cloud on SAP BTP and bring them to market via SAP Store. These products run on SAP BTP and can integrate with SAP S/4HANA, other SAP solutions and non-SAP solutions.

To support this journey, SAP BTP ABAP Environment Product Management offers a free engagement for partners who want to build and commercialize products on SAP BTP ABAP Environment.

Who is this for?

This engagement is intended for SAP partners who:

  • want to offer a product to multiple customers
  • are planning a side-by-side solution on SAP BTP instead of an on-stack SAP S/4HANA extension
  • are considering SAP BTP ABAP Environment as their product platform
  • want to publish their solution via SAP Store

To commercialize and publish a partner product via SAP Store, an SAP PartnerEdge Build agreement is required. If you are not yet part of SAP PartnerEdge Build, we can discuss the required next steps in the initial conversation.

Supported delivery options

We currently support two delivery options for partner products on SAP BTP ABAP Environment.

Multitenant SaaS: The partner offers and operates the solution as a cloud service. Customers subscribe to the service and receive their own tenant in the partner's provider system.

Add-On Product: The partner offers the solution as software. Customers run their own SAP BTP ABAP Environment instance and consume the partner product in their own landscape, with the option to extend it according to their needs.

Both options enable partners to productize cloud solutions depending on their target customers, operating model and business model. Partners can also offer both delivery options in parallel, as most of the underlying steps are identical.

How we support you

As part of the engagement, SAP BTP ABAP Environment Product Management can support you with:

  • choosing the right delivery option
  • validating your product scenario and architecture
  • answering technical questions during implementation
  • sharing reference materials and tutorials
  • preparing your solution for SAP Store publication
  • moving towards go-live

The goal is to help partners bring their products live on SAP BTP ABAP Environment faster.

Interested?

If your company is planning to build a partner product on SAP BTP ABAP Environment, we would be happy to hear from you.

Please contact us: sap_btp_abap_environment@sap.com

To help us prepare the first discussion, please include a short description of your product idea or existing solution, your target customers, current development stage, and preferred delivery option if already known.

We will then set up a remote session to better understand your scenario and discuss how we can support your journey.

 

Helpful resources

To get started, you may find the following resources useful: 

  • Building partner products on SAP BTP ABAP Environment
  • Overview of delivery options: Multitenant SaaS and Add-On Product
  • Tutorial: MT SaaS Partner Reference Application
  • PE Build onboarding

entry.title: Utilities CX Reloaded: Discover the New Sales Process in Our July 7 Espresso Session
entry.content:
Dear Utilities Community,

We are delighted to invite you to another Espresso Session in our popular 30-minute format, where we will introduce our new Utilities CX sales process and how it helps utilities simplify and modernize sales operations in deregulated energy markets.

Discover how customer service and sales agents can initiate sales workflows directly from the SAP Sales and Service Cloud desktop, create tailored utility sales orders with relevant product recommendations, and seamlessly integrate front-office activities with SAP S/4HANA Utilities processes.

You'll also see how guided energy sales brings previously fragmented process steps together into a single, end-to-end workflow, enabling greater efficiency, faster responses to customer needs, and improved sales performance.

 

Key Topics

✅SAP Sales Cloud and Service Cloud Version 2 for Utilities
Learn about the benefits of the utilities add-on and how it supports modern utility sales processes.

✅Utility Use Cases
See how utilities can enhance sales and customer engagement through deep integration with back-end systems and business processes.

✅Live Demo
Experience a streamlined, end-to-end sales workflow in action and see how SAP solutions work together to support utility sales teams.

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Who Should Join?

This session is intended for professionals in utilities working in sales and account management, customer service and contact center leaders, CX, CRM, and business process experts, and IT and SAP architecture who want to simplify the sales processes across the organization.

 
If you are looking to simplify sales processes, improve customer interactions, and drive greater efficiency across your organization, this session is for you.
 
Our speakers Tijana Mattoo and Steen Hansen are happy to answer your questions. 
 
Please register here for our session taking place on
📅Tuesday, July 7, 2026
🕓4:00 - 4:30 PM CEST
 
We look forward to seeing you there! ☕

entry.title: Re: Compensation Promotion with Position Management Not E...

Hello Community,

Is it required to Lean Cloud application programming language to every functional consultant in this Cloud and AI era.

If yes how to start and if basic understanding required could you please share the links so that can prepare and start the learning.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: Re: URGENT – C_THR81_2605 SyBA scores 15% both attempts,...
entry.content:
Dear @MoncefMarjo,
Thank you for your detailed explanation.
We are aware of the current technical issues affecting some learners and understand that this does not meet the experience we strive to provide. Please be assured that our experts are working diligently to resolve these issues and restore stability as quickly as possible. For any sessions that were impacted, exam attempts will be regranted where appropriate.

Thank you for your patience, understanding, and continued commitment to learning with SAP.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Solution for Attachment Replication from SAP Business Suite to sales cloud V2?
entry.content:
The standard Replicate Sales Order and Sales Quote from SAP S4HANA iFlow only covers header, products, prices, and parties "no attachments".

Is there a standard SAP iFlow/content package for replicating S/4 sales order/quote attachments to Sales Cloud V2 (similar to the old C4C AttachmentFolderReplicationRequest_Out scenario)? Or is a custom post-exit using API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV → V2 AttachmentFolder the only way? or there is any other way to handle this integration

Has anyone built this in a live project?

Many Thanks,

Ruchita

entry.title: Copy item texts from requisition to purchase order
entry.content: While creating PO with reference to PR, item texts in PR are not copied to PO

entry.title: Re: Compensation Promotion with Position Management Not E...
entry.content: The job must be created first. If the manager wants to create a new position, or, promote someone to a position that is already filled (i.e. you have the maximum of 4 district managers and need a 5th), the position needs to be added/updated prior to the compensation action.

entry.title: Re: Changing the unit of measure during RF packing in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Great one @Shubh07 !

I am seeing the following behavior.

System is expecting the quantity value (qty verification) to be filled in, only then SF2 (UoMC) will work/hit the function and enable the UOM field to be editable. This causes additional scan for RF operator. If user decides to change UOM (to alt UOM) for the pallet in the first place since he is in front of the pallet, then he is still forced to

  1. Scan the quantity.
  2. Sf2.
  3. Update altuom.
  4. Scan the quantity in alt UOM.
  5. Complete the move to dest HU.

Were you also able to notice the same behavior?

Thanks,

Sreeram G

entry.title: Customer Retention over acquisition - What the SAP Advanced Success Plan offers utilities customers
entry.content:
The utilities industry is undergoing its most significant transformation in decades. The shift to renewables, smart metering, distributed energy resources, and expanding deregulation are reshaping the relationship between utility companies and their customers. Customer experience is no longer a back-office concern, it is a front-line competitive differentiator.

The SAP Advanced Success Plan for Customer Experience is designed precisely for this moment. It offers utilities customers a structured, continuously guided approach to maximizing value from SAP CX investments; from first adoption through to AI-enabled optimization. In this post, we walk through every available service, explain what each one does, and connect it to the realities utilities organizations face today.

A Sector in Motion: Why Customer Experience Has Become Strategic for Utilities

Four converging forces are making customer experience a boardroom priority for utilities and increasing the demand for structured, expert-led adoption support.

Energy transition and service complexity: Renewables, EV charging, solar feed-in tariffs, and demand response programs are adding new service dimensions. Customers expect their energy provider to manage this complexity seamlessly.

Deregulation and customer switching: In liberalized energy markets, customers can, and do, switch providers. The cost of acquisition consistently exceeds the cost of retention. Superior service experience is a measurable retention lever.

Smart metering and data volume: Smart meter rollouts generate billions of interval readings daily. This data can fuel proactive outreach and personalized billing, but only if the CX platform is correctly configured to act on it.

Regulatory intensity: Billing accuracy mandates, complaint resolution timelines, and outage notification requirements are intensifying. The SAP CX platform supports compliance, but only when features are correctly activated and configured.

SAP in Utilities: A Significant and Growing Market

Utilities are not a niche segment for SAP. Understanding the scale of the SAP utilities community frames why the Advanced Success Plan matters for this industry in particular. SAP is trusted by hundreds of utilities customers globally and is among the leading technology providers in the sector by installed base, a position that continues to grow year-on-year. The investment in the relationship has been made, the question is whether customers are fully using what they have.

“With the Advanced Success Plan, customers turn their SAP Customer Experience into a driver of growth and innovation. They gain the confidence to act decisively, supported by unlimited expert guidance and intelligent insights that ensure every feature delivers measurable business value.”

The Business Case: Customer Retention Over Acquisition

For utilities operating in competitive markets, retaining customers has become as strategically important as acquiring new ones. Customers can switch supplier in minutes via a digital platform; complaint escalation processes are visible on social media; and billing errors in a smart meter world are harder to excuse and faster to escalate.

The SAP CX platform addresses these challenges across the full customer lifecycle. SAP Engagement Cloud enables targeted, segmented communications. SAP Service Cloud centralizes complaint management and agent interactions. SAP Sales Cloud for managing accounts, contacts, leads, and Opportunities and SAP Commerce Cloud is the digital sales and self-service storefront layer; it handles how customers discover, compare, purchase, and manage energy products online. What the Advanced Success Plan does is ensure these capabilities are not just purchase; they are adopted, optimized, and continuously improved.

How the Advanced Success Plan Structures the Adoption Journey

The Advanced Success Plan organizes the adoption journey across four distinct phases, each with a defined purpose and a set of targeted services:

Introducing Innovation: Identify and priorities the right innovations for your business. Services here include product guidance sessions covering areas such as Utilities Session and Agent Desktop, Intelligent Selling, Campaigns and Segmentation, and AI-Foundation and Use case Navigator.

Adopting Innovation: Plan and prepare for structured adoption with minimal risk. Services include the AI Process Fit-Gap Analysis, Key Feature Advisory, Release Guidance, Success Expert engagement, Service Planning, Value Management, Innovation Checkpoints, and Adoption Checkpoints.

Activation and Optimization: Hands-on activation of AI and CX capabilities in your environment. Services include activation sessions across Sales, Service, and Marketing and Engagement clouds; AI Agent Activation; Embedded AI Activation; Joule Activation; and Technical and Functional Assistance.

Success Measurement and Continuous Improvement: Measure outcomes and sustain momentum through ongoing engagement. Services include a Continuous Engagement model, Release Guidance, Success Expert, Value Management, Innovation Checkpoints, and Adoption Checkpoints.

The Eight Services Available to Utilities Customers — and How Each One Helps

The Advanced Success Plan for Customer Experience comprises eight distinct services. Each has a defined scope, a specific business need it addresses, and measurable benefits. For utilities customers, each service connects to a characteristic operational or strategic challenge.

01 — Product Guidance for SAP Customer Experience Solutions 
Structured sessions that introduce utilities teams to the capabilities most relevant to their context; from meter-read-driven billing notifications in SAP Service Cloud to segmented communications in SAP Engagement Cloud. The goal is to accelerate time-to-awareness: teams know what exists before they have to find it.

02 — Key Feature Advisory for SAP Customer Experience Solutions 
Curated, customer-specific guidance on which features to activate and in what sequence. For utilities, this means filtering a broad SAP CX roadmap down to the capabilities that matter for smart metering, complaints management, and outage communications then discarding what does not apply.

03 — Release Guidance for SAP Customer Experience Solutions
Every SAP CX quarterly release brings dozens of updates. Release Guidance ensures utilities teams receive a focused brief on what is relevant to their industry context, not a generic list of all product changes, so adoption decisions are faster and better informed.

04 — Solution Review for SAP Customer Experience Solutions 
A structured review of the current solution configuration against best practice. For utilities, this commonly surfaces configuration gaps in complaint workflows, billing notification templates, or service agent desktop layouts that erode the customer experience over time if left unaddressed.

05 — Transformation Advisory for SAP Customer Experience Solutions 
Strategic guidance that connects SAP CX capabilities to the outcome’s utilities care about most: cost-to-serve reduction, complaint resolution improvement, and regulatory compliance. Transformation Advisory moves the conversation from features to business impact.

06 — Activation for SAP Customer Experience Solutions
Hands-on, expert-assisted activation of specific capabilities in the customer's live environment. For utilities, this includes activating Joule for service agents, enabling AI-driven case categorization in Service Cloud. Many autonomous agents or assistants announced by SAP in Sapphire , once available can be activated to guide service agents to process cases.

07 — Technical Assistance for SAP Customer Experience Solutions
Direct technical support across all phases of the engagement — covering integration architecture, performance guidance, load testing guidance for mass billing cycles, and resolution of complex system behavior. This service is especially critical for utilities given the high-volume, time-sensitive nature of meter-read processing and month-end billing runs.

08 — Functional Assistance for SAP Customer Experience
Business process and functional configuration support throughout the engagement. Utilities-specific coverage includes complaint handling workflows, outage notification sequences, and the configuration of billing determinant displays in the service agent desktop, ensuring that what is built serves the way utilities actually operate.

From Idea to Impact: How the Advanced Success Plan AI Services Support Utilities Across Every Phase

Artificial intelligence is not a future aspiration for utilities — it is a present operational reality. Smart meter data, deregulated market dynamics, rising customer expectations, and regulatory complexity all create conditions where AI can deliver measurable value. But AI adoption is not a single event. It is a journey that runs from initial discovery through to continuous optimization in production. The Advanced Success Plan AI Services Catalogue provides a structured set of twelve services that guide utilities customers through exactly that journey — from understanding what is possible to running AI at scale.

 

A Practitioner's Perspective: What I See in the Field

Working directly with utilities customers, I see first-hand how the Advanced Success Plan services drive meaningful business value — especially for organizations without deep technical or functional SAP CX expertise in-house. Utility companies face a specific set of challenges: complex billing architectures, regulatory requirements, and high customer expectations for responsive service. The right services, delivered at the right moment, can be genuinely transformational. Four patterns stand out consistently.

Bridging the knowledge gap for business users: Unlike system integrators or technical consultants, many utility business teams may not fully grasp the functional nuances required to maximize their SAP investment. Value-driven CSM engagement becomes critical here: translating technical possibilities into business outcomes, advising on feature adoption, and ensuring cross-team alignment across operations, IT, and customer management. The CSM acts as connective tissue between what SAP CX can do and what the business needs to achieve.

Go-live checks: foundations for seamless launches: Utilities cannot rely on generic go-live checklists. Are meter reads flowing end-to-end? Are billing determinants and rate structures properly mapped? Are customer service orders integrated with billing and meter management systems? Comprehensive go-live checks built for the utilities context eliminate the guesswork — giving teams confidence that key processes are functioning correctly from day one, not discovered to be broken three months into the first billing cycle.

Roadmap guidance: enabling better design decisions: Each SAP CX release brings dozens of new features, but only subsets are relevant for a given customer in a given market. Regular, curated roadmap guidance helps utilities focus on the features and design decisions that will most efficiently drive their specific outcomes — whether that is improving customer satisfaction, streamlining operations, or enriching digital self-service. This targeted approach prevents costly missteps and empowers business stakeholders to make confident decisions without requiring deep product expertise of their own.

Early-phase support: building for reliability and performance: The early project phase is often when performance issues and integration risks are best addressed — but it is also where internal teams feel least certain. Proactive support that includes performance reviews spanning interconnected billing and service systems, high-level architecture guidance, and utilities-specific load testing helps ensure reliable operation under real-world peak conditions. Utilities processing millions of daily meter reads, running mass billing cycles at month-end, and handling seasonal demand spikes need to know their system will hold before the peak arrives, not after it has passed.

What Stronger SAP CX Adoption Looks Like in Practice

When utilities customers engage with the full Advanced Success Plan service portfolio at the right moments, the outcomes are concrete and measurable:

Adoption confidence: Teams understand the SAP CX capabilities that matter for their industry and know how to use them. Adoption is driven by guided enablement, not trial and error.

Configuration quality: Solution reviews and functional assistance ensure complaint workflows, billing notifications, and service processes are correctly configured — reducing workarounds and support volume.

Release relevance: Every quarterly SAP CX update is assessed for utilities relevance. Teams receive a focused brief on what to adopt — not a generic list of all product changes.

AI activation at pace: Joule, embedded AI agents, and Engagement cloud intelligence features are activated with expert assistance — moving from available-in-catalogue to live-in-production for utilities use cases.

Integration reliability: Standard integrations between SAP Service Cloud and SAP S/4HANA Utilities are correctly configured, giving agents real-time access to meter history, billing data, and service orders.

Transformation clarity: Transformation advisory connects the SAP CX roadmap to the outcomes utilities care about most: cost-to-serve reduction, complaint resolution improvement, and regulatory compliance.

A Service Portfolio Built for the Complexity of Utilities

The SAP investment in the utilities sector is substantial and well-established. Utilities organisations around the world have already deployed the SAP CX platform and are running their customer operations on it. The question is not whether they have access to world-class CX capabilities — it is whether they are fully using what they have.

The eight services described in this post, combined with CSM-led perspectives on go-live quality, roadmap relevance, and performance assurance, represent a comprehensive framework for utilities organizations to realize the full potential of their SAP CX investment. The Advanced Success Plan is not a generic offering. When applied with industry focus and CSM expertise, it becomes a strategic asset — one that helps utilities deliver on the promise of a superior customer experience in an increasingly competitive, regulated, and technically complex market.

Are you a utilities customer working through SAP CX adoption or optimization? We would welcome more conversation in the comments — whether it is a specific challenge you are navigating, a service in this portfolio you have found valuable, or a question about where to start.

entry.title: Re: The future vision of Authorization Management in SAP Cloud ALM - Enterprise-Grade Access Control
entry.content:
Great!

Maybe this gives the chance to introduce a big improvement in SAP's authorization concept: Add to the allowance a denial layer.

I can't even begin to describe all the click-and-type-frenzy sessions I've had to endure in the past, just because I wanted to allow almost everything—except for one particularly sensitive item—in a PFCG role.

As you are redesigning all, the door to this big improvement is open!

entry.title: Re: Define bank transfer tile App id: F3759 - Custom Fiel...
entry.content: According to the official app documentation, this feature is supported via SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects. I recommend consulting with your technical development team to explore this extensibility option further.

entry.title: Re: Eligibility rule workaround in Non-EC instance
entry.content: We actually have standard fields like CompensationEligible and MeritEligible that can be used in the UDF. But the answer is the same, the use of a UDF field, custom or one I just mentioned, is the most efficient way to solve this issue.

entry.title: Re: Customer Retention over acquisition - What the SAP Advanced Success Plan offers utilities custom
entry.content: Great perspective and practical guidance on how customers can succeed with CX solutions.

entry.title: Re: Quick Start guide for PLM system integration 3.0 Implementation

Sumit

entry.title: Re: Your Sherlock Homes - How to Find Missing Business Catalogs?
entry.content:
Bringing this one back up because I wanted to ask if anyone has successfully used SAP Note 2975653 recently for changes in business catalogs?

I’m curious if any steps have changed since George’s post. Also, if you've found other helpful resources or tips for working with the Fiori Apps Reference Library, I'd love to hear them!

entry.title: Re: Your Sherlock Homes - How to Find Missing Business Catalogs?
entry.content: You can also check out the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library to track down any missing business catalogs you need. It’s helpful for figuring out which roles you might need.

entry.title: Re: How to Set Automatically “Print via Output Control" i...
entry.content: I don't think that is correct.

entry.title: Re: How to Set Automatically “Print via Output Control" i...
entry.content:
Hello @kumarArnob_Mandol 

In your scenario GR is actually posted by inbound delivery confirmation sent by the interface from EWM. That is processed with a communication user. Therefore you need to set NDR for that communication user.

You also need to maintain output determination - SPRO: Materials Management -> Inventory Management and Physical Inventory -> Output Determination -> ...

I would suggest to run exactly the same process of inbound delivery receipt but with a storage location that is not EWM managed. This way you can diagnose output determination. Once you get the GR slip printed, move to EWM managed storage location.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Replication Flow - Projection Functions
entry.content: Hello Martin, thank you for your comment. May I ask in this blog what is the purpose of the function column in the 'mapping' tab of the projection? I was also not able to use till now.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Assign Collection Management Attributes via...
entry.content: Hi Rohit, Chris, We are experiencing the same issue. Is there a better solution available in the meantime or do we need to create a job for each day? Please let me know! Kind regards, Ian

entry.title: Re: Create Third-Party and Standard Inbound Delivery Notification via Webservice
entry.content:
I know this thread is old but the InboundDeliveryProcessing webservice could fill a need we have.

I can successfully create a (partial) Delivery Notification against a To-Stock PO, which is Advised, Consistent, Not Started and Not Released.

My question is: is there a way, using this webservice, to complete the process and Release the Inbound Delivery (so that the goods receipt is completed)?
Thanks,
Eelco

entry.title: Re: Copy item texts from requisition to purchase order
entry.content: You need to verify text determination configuration - SPRO Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Purchase Order -> Texts for Purchase Orders -> ...

entry.title: Complete Beginner Seeking Guidance to Become an SAP ABAP Developer
entry.content:
I'm a final-year engineering student and a complete beginner to SAP. I don't have any prior knowledge of SAP, but I'm highly motivated to learn and build a career as an SAP ABAP Developer.

 

I recently got access to the SAP Learning Hub, Student Edition, and I'm a bit confused about where to begin. There are many learning journeys and courses available, so I'd really appreciate some guidance.

 

Could you please suggest:

 

- The best learning path for a complete beginner who wants to become an SAP ABAP Developer.

- Which courses or learning journeys I should complete first.

- Any hands-on practice resources, projects, or tips that helped you when you started.

 

Thank you in advance for your guidance and support!

entry.title: Re: Eligibility rule workaround in Non-EC instance

entry.title: Hash-Based Load Balancing in BTP Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
• Introduction to Load Balancing in BTP Cloud Foundry
  • Use Cases for Hash-Based Load Balancing
  • Sample Application for a Hash-Based Load Balancing
  • Hash-Based Load Balancing in Action
  • References

Introduction to Load Balancing in BTP Cloud Foundry 

Requests to applications running on the BTP CF can be routed using different load balancing strategies. The default strategy is round-robin, which evenly distributes requests across application instances. The least-connection load balancing directs requests to the application instances with the fewest active connections first. However, these algorithms are not ideal for scenarios that require consistent routing behavior. 

A hash-based load balancing algorithm was recently introduced to tackle such scenarios. It allows you to define an HTTP request header containing a specific identifier – such as a tenant ID, a user ID, or a resource ID – to route requests to the same subset of application instances. Selection of a suitable load balancing strategy for your Cloud Foundry application depends on your use case. 

This blog post provides an overview of the hash-based load balancing algorithm in Cloud Foundry, covering its use cases and a step-by-step walkthrough of configuring it for a sample application. 

Use Cases for Hash-Based Load Balancing  

Hash-based load balancing addresses a class of Cloud Foundry application constraints related to backing service limitations – such as finite database connection pools or in-memory caches. It consistently routes requests to the same subset of instances, enabling optimization in scenarios where those instances maintain stateful resources. 

One use case is when users from different tenants send requests that are spread across all available instances, requiring each instance to establish connections to multiple tenant-specific databases. Instead, the hash-based load balancing algorithm routes each tenant's traffic to a consistent subset of instances, allowing those instances to minimize database connection overhead. This provides a flexible and scalable approach beyond traditional session affinity. 

By pinning tenants, users, or resources to application instances based on an HTTP request header, this algorithm helps to optimize cache usage, reduce latency, and avoid redundant database connections. 

Sample Application for a Hash-Based Load Balancing 

To use the hash-based load balancing, you need to ensure that you are using BTP CF release 2606 or higher. 

Hash-based load balancing is configured at the application route level. The configuration can be easily provided in the application manifest or directly via CF CLI commands "cf create-route", "cf update-route", and "cf map-route". 

If a hash header for a hash-based route is not present in the HTTP request, the platform's default load balancing algorithm, round-robin, applies. 

Let’s create and deploy a sample app with a hash-based balanced route and see how requests are routed to specific application instances. 

You can clone the Cloud Foundry routing samples repository. Our sample app is in the per-route/loadbalancing folder, where you can find manifest.yml structured as follows: 

- name: per-route-loadbalancing-demo 
 
  routes: 
    - route: per-route-lb-hb.<domain> 
      options: 
        loadbalancing: hash 
        hash_header: tenant-id 
        hash_balance: 1.2

In the routes section for our test application, the "<domain>" must be replaced with the actual domain available in your BTP CF subaccount. It can be retrieved via the CF CLI command "cf domains" or via the BTP Cockpit. 

The hash balance route option defines a balance factor. The value 1.2 means that no single application instance should handle more than 120% of the average number of in-flight requests across all instances managed by the Gorouter. In-flight requests exceeding this threshold will be redirected to other less-loaded application instances. 
 
A hash balance factor of 0 (or leaving it undefined) means that all requests for a particular hash are routed to the same instance if it is healthy.  

You can push the app via CF CLI command "cf push -f manifest.yml". 

Execute "cf app per-route-loadbalancing-demo" to verify that three instances of the application are running successfully. If so, the “cf routes” command should display a new route "per-route-lb-hb.<domain>" in your CF organization and indicate that your route is mapped to the test app "per-route-loadbalancing-demo". 

Hash-Based Load Balancing in Action 

You can now curl the app, specifying a tenant ID in the HTTP request header named "tenant-id". As you may recall, we configured this name in the app manifest. The following examples target the /requests endpoint of the test application with different hash header values. 

        curl -s https://per-route-lb-hb.<domain>/requests -H "tenant-id: 0" 
        curl -s https://per-route-lb-hb.<domain>/requests -H "tenant-id: 1" 

To verify that the requests are routed to specific application instances based on the tenant-id, you can execute "cf logs per-route-loadbalancing-demo" in a separate terminal and inspect the "app_index" of the responses in real time. You should observe that all the requests with the same tenant-id are handled by the same application instance. 

This simple test aims to give you a first impression of hash-based routing.  To summarize, you have deployed an app with a route configured for the hash-based load balancing algorithm and have seen it in action for a simple scenario with multiple application instances and a single hash header. 

References  

All the technical details of hash-based load balancing can be found in the Cloud Foundry documentation.
A good deep dive into BTP load balancing can be found in the article “Support for Customizable Load Balancing Algorithms”.

entry.title: Organizational Unit Not Appearing in F4 Help for AB Partner Function in Quality Notification
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I created a new Organizational Unit using transaction PPOCE. However, when I create/change a Quality Notification (QM01/QM02) and try to assign the Org Unit to partner function AB – Department Responsible, the newly created Org Unit does not appear in the F4 value help.

I have already verified that the Org Unit was created successfully in PPOCE.

Could you please advise which configuration or settings should be checked to ensure that newly created Org Units are available in the F4 help for partner function AB?
However, if I try to assign the Org unit value manually, it accepts.

Specifically, I would like to know:

  • Does the Org Unit need to be assigned to a particular organizational hierarchy or relationship?
  • Are there any HR OM tables/relationships that need to be maintained or updated?
  • Is there any buffer refresh or consistency check required after creating a new Org Unit?

System: [S/4HANA 2023]

I really appreciate any help you can provide.

Best Regards,

entry.content: Static groups could work but it seems like a lot more administrative work than the other option.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Identity Services Bug in SAP BTP Trial Environment: Current Status
entry.content:
Hello @Maria_Radde  may i know any update about the  issue going on? It's already  more than one month right.

entry.title: Re: Get Started with SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition!
entry.content:
Hi team,

I'm using this tool since first version. Interesting and always more rich about informations.

I approve the new version of extraction tool, organized by process KPI: one job for each KPI. But, to complete the RC_VALUE_DISCOVERY_COLL_DATA job I need more than 1 hour, when the total execution time of the KPI Job needs only 10 minutes. There is a WAIT UP TO 60 SECONDS statement that, for the most of KPI, is oversized.

I suggest to reduce it or manage this timing by a technical parameter. When the tool is implemented in a SAP ERP landscape (ECC or S/4HANA is un-relevant), to test the tool and execute it in production system, we need almost 3 hours.

Best regards

Simone

entry.title: Error while posting asset retirement
entry.content:
While posting asset retirement entries through the Post Asset Retirement app, we are receiving an error stating that G/L account 0030100017 is locked for Asset Class Z001.

However, the same G/L account 0030100017 is assigned to other asset classes, and asset retirement postings are completed successfully without any error for those classes.

The same issue was also observed for Asset Class Z002.

I am sharing the screenshots of the Asset Account Determination key settings along with the asset posting error message for your reference. Kindly review and check the issue.

entry.title: Re: Material Documents Overview app key features - S/4HANA Public Cloud

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Summary
entry.content: Hi @aelghannam , done - the OData service is now updated (the rest of the stats will remain the same).

entry.title: Re: Cloud Application Programming For SAP Functional Cons...
entry.content:
Dear @Ashish13,

This helps functional consultants understand whether a requirement should be solved by standard configuration, key-user extensibility, developer extensibility, SAP Build, integration or a side-by-side CAP extension

Suggested learning path:

SAP Learning – Exploring SAP Business Technology Platform
SAP Learning – SAP Build
SAP Learning – Developing with SAP Integration Suite
SAP Help – Developing with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
SAP Learning – Introduction to SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
SAP Learning – Building Side-by-Side Extensions on SAP BTP
SAP Learning – Artificial Intelligence at SAP Learning

Summary:

Deep CAP coding is not required for every functional consultant but a basic conceptual understanding is highly recommended. It will help you stay relevant in SAP cloud projects and work more effectively with technical, integration and BTP teams

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Eligibility rule workaround in Non-EC instance
entry.content: One more and the last question regarding this case: in UDF file I see at least 2 compensation eligibility related fields: COMPENSATION_ELIGIBLE & COMPENSATION_SALARY_ELIGIBLE. The expected values should be True for eligible and False for ineligible employees, right? And what's the conceptual difference between those two fields? I don't use stock & bonus tabs for in my comp template. In this case, in which of those two fields should I populate (true, false) values?

entry.title: Re: EWM- Packing slip Auto Email through Post Processing Framework.
entry.content:
Hi,

In case of dynamic recipient i.e. Mail address from BP of ship to party or sold to party then static recipient won't work. In that case how do you configure?

Regards,

Ganesh

entry.title: Re: GET Parameter ID in Update-Function-Module
entry.content:
If you have your Z report like this:

REPORT zreport.
SET PARAMETER ID 'ZZ' FIELD 'VALUE'.
CALL TRANSACTION 'CO02' MODE 'E'...

and CO02 does:

CALL FUNCTION 'CO_VB_ORDER_PRINT' IN UPDATE TASK...
...
COMMIT WORK. " triggers all CALL FUNCTION IN UPDATE TASK asynchronously by default

an update task is created by default (unless SET UPDATE TASK LOCAL  runs). It corresponds to a brand new User Session which doesn't access the same memory context. Ref: ABAP Keyword Documentation - Sessions and Memory Areas.

Several solutions:

  1. Force the update task to run in the same User Session via CALL TRANSACTION 'CO02' UPDMODE 'L' (it's equivalent to run SET UPDATE TASK LOCAL at the start of the new user session).
  2. If you need to keep the synchronous or local execution of the update task, you may also pass anything to the memory of the update task by creating a Z update function module which does SET PARAMETER ID 'ZZ' FIELD 'VALUE', calling this function module via CALL FUNCTION 'ZYOURFM' IN UPDATE TASK EXPORTING zz = value before CALL FUNCTION 'CO_VB_ORDER_PRINT' IN UPDATE TASK
  3. As already said, you may use any other solution which stores data in a persistent storage, but the solution is more complex if you want to handle several users running the program at the same time.

entry.title: Re: URGENT – C_THR81_2605 SyBA scores 15% both attempts,...
entry.content: Hi Margit, Thank you for the update and for confirming this is being looked into — really appreciate you following up personally. Kind regards, Moncef

entry.title: Re: Configuration of table T510S - Integration between Time Management and Payroll
entry.content: same can we use for negetive time management.

entry.title: How to restrict access to specific cost centers in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: I am struggling to see if we can restrict user so that only the cost centers which relates to them are visible when raising purchase requisition. Is there a way to restrict so that the user dosesnt see all the cost centers and only relevant ones.

entry.title: Re: GET Parameter ID in Update-Function-Module
entry.content:
Hello @master1980 

The parameter ID is empty because SET/GET parameters don’t work across update tasks.
Your SmartForm runs in an update task (different LUW/session), so it cannot access the GET parameter set earlier

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Limitations with Business Rule - Benefit Employee Claim SuccessFactors
entry.content:
This is being added in the Q/A Section to grab the attention of the Product Experts and the Engineering Team. Please find the issue described in detailed below

So, One of my Clients wanted to have the petty cash process automated through the SuccessFactors and We went ahead with the Benefit Employee Claim - Reimbursement Type. There are two categories of Reimbursement, Petty Cash Advance and Petty Cash Expense. Petty Cash Advance is applied first against which Petty Cash Expense is later raised once the expenses are done. Unique Reference numbers are generated for both Petty Cash Advance and Expenses for tracking and reporting purposes. When Employees raise Petty Cash Expense against advance received , they are required to enter the Petty Cash Advance Reference Number mandatorily. To make the proces efficient , convenient and error - free , we automated the petty cash advance reference number  by auto population (User selects the date on which the petty cash advance was raised) and reference number is auto-populated in the request. This was done using a Lookup Rule.

Adding more context to this , This is a multiple proof yes Benefit (Benefit Employee Claim Detail Object is being used) to allow the employees to book expenses under multiple categories and Total Amount is populated and is a ready only field (multiple proof yes makes the total amount field read only).This is where the issues arises, where SAP has claimed that any rule triggered after total amount field is filled would make the total amount field reset to 0 (Only in the UI) , however it is fine in the UI's and amount claimed gets added to the accumulation Object. This is a very confusing for the end - users as they would mistake the 0 , not having this rule would make the process tiresome as they have to manually enter the request number which is inconvenient for the end users and is error-prone. Attaching screenshot of the rule below

Hoping someone from the Product Engineering Team works on this and get this resolved as rule triggers after total amount resetting the field to 0 is a concerning limitation.I am open for any discussions on this !

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI to Microsoft SharePoint Online – Certificate-...
entry.content:
Hello

Custom JWT generation in CPI is the recommended and widely used solution.

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Filters not propagating to Data Analyzer across linke...
entry.content:
Hello @Wisam_Alsalti 

Filters are dropped because Data Analyzer does not support cross-model (linked model) filters. Only direct filters on a single model are carried over

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: upgrade Flexso TDD integration suite environment to enhanced edition
entry.content:
We would like to upgrade our TDD Integration Suite environment to "enhanced edition", in order to get hands-on access to new MCP gateway component. 
What is the procedure to get this going?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud | CO11N Goods Movement Valid...
entry.content:
Hello @suryakant_pandeyd3934e 

Deletion detection: Not possible at item-level BAdI alone (deleted lines never come).
→ Compare reservation/BOM (I_ProductionOrderComponent / I_ReservationDocumentItem) vs posted items and raise error if any missing.

Fetch full expected components (reservation/BOM)
Compare with material document items
If mismatch (missing/zero qty) → raise error


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Eligibility rule workaround in Non-EC instance
entry.content: COMPENSATION_ELIGIBLE dictates if the employee appears on the form. If this is FALSE, no other eligibility field matter. COMPENSATION_SALARY_ELIGIBLE indicates if the tab is active for the employee. If FALSE, the row will be grayed out and no information will be shown other than the name. If TRUE, the row is active and able to plan. The last level is field level. For example, MERIT_ELIGIBLE is FALSE and the other levels are true, the employee appears on the form, the salary row is visible but the merit fields, including guidelines are grayed out. There are similar fields for EXTRA, EXTRA2, etc.

entry.title: Re: Hierarchical Entity Selection in Predictive Planning
entry.content:
@francois_imberton thanks, looking forward to this being deployed in other areas.
The related IR's are already there, for example: 278541 + 279199 + 332528 + ...

entry.title: Re: BI Report Development for Job Families (Employee Cent...
entry.content: Appreciate the response. The Source is SAP Success Factors, with an API extraction into SAP BW. Within SAC, these 3 dimensions are from a single BW query used in a Live Connection. The Dimensions are not working as expected.

entry.title: Re: Upcoming Change: Member Limit Enforcement for Data Action Parameters
entry.content:
I've seen that when setting just a single hierarchy node in a data action parameter (manually or through script, like a top node from a large dimension) that in the Data Action monitor we see the fully expanded list (selected node + descendant subnodes + leave mebers underneath). This could easily reach 1000+ members.
Will this scenario be impacted or not?

entry.title: Re: KSII - Maessage KD301
entry.content: Thanks so much.

entry.title: SAP Ariba Certification Roadmap for Beginners (Student Edition)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am a verified student using SAP Learning Hub, Student Edition, and I am currently learning SAP Ariba through practical training.

I would like guidance from experienced SAP Ariba professionals on the complete learning and certification roadmap.

  1. Which SAP Ariba Learning Journey should I start with as a beginner?
  2. Which certification should I complete first (foundation level)?
  3. After the beginner certification, what certifications are recommended for intermediate and advanced levels?
  4. Is there a recommended sequence of SAP Ariba certifications for someone who wants to become an SAP Ariba Functional Consultant?
  5. Are there any certifications or learning journeys that are considered essential by employers in the SAP Ariba domain?

I would appreciate your recommendations on the best official SAP Learning path from beginner to advanced.

Thank you!

entry.title: Applying Retirement Eligibility for Talent search SUCCESSION
entry.content:
Hi experts, hope you are doing fine

Client requested to be able to use a filter on the talent search (same as we have for Impact of Loss or Reason for Leaving) for the retirement eligibility (yes/no)

So the rules would be based on "Retirement Eligibility Settings" for example,

Hope you can help,

Thank you!

entry.title: Issue with oauth authentication while calling Integration flow
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I have created one Oauth clinent and also added the role ESBMesseging.send role to it, i can fetch the access token but not able to call the endpoint iflow of https in CPI and getting 403 error

But what about VarPay bonus programs? seems we do not have dedicated eligibility fields in UDF for bonus. Especially when a several bonus programs should be implemented (different VarPay Templates) and each of this has it own eligibility logic (parameters)

entry.title: 中国本地化收付款管理
entry.content:
一. 业务场景

付款申请•查看待付款项•发起付款申请-付款审批•设置审批规则•审核付款申请•批准/拒绝付款-付款策略•指定付款方式•触发付款执行-付款执行•银行转账•发送付款报文•跟踪付款状态•汇票•汇票背书转让•开立应付汇票

entry.title: Inquiry regarding Form Extensibility
entry.content:
Hi support,

Here I have an inquiry regarding the form extensibility for the below forms:
Order Data List form (PP_MOR_DATA_LIST) - FDP_PP_MOR_DATA_LIST_SRV
Sales Scheduling Agreement Print Form (SD_SLS_SCHEDULING_AGREEMENT) -FDP_PP_MOR_DATA_LIST_SRV.

The requirements are to extend multiple fields to the respective forms that do not have released table nor standard CDS to extend.

The method we could proceed is by using Custom App with custom CDS Views.

We will like to know the best practice suggest by SAP to proceed with this inquiry.

Thank you.

 

Regards,
Neoh

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI to Microsoft SharePoint Online – Certificate-...
entry.content: Is Custom JWT was SAP suggested?

entry.title: Re: How do we delete Project demand
entry.content: Hi @dawid90, thank you for your response. Only wanted to clear some things How can a sub-WBS element be created in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, and what impact does it have when a demand is assigned to it? Specifically, does assigning a demand to a sub-WBS element affect any FI/CO (Finance and Controlling) processes in an investment project? Additionally, how does the demand function when it is assigned to a sub-WBS, and what is the relationship between the main WBS element and the sub-WBS element in terms of cost collection, reporting, and settlement? In our scenario, demands are primarily used for labour (resource) planning. Could you also clarify any implications specific to this setup?

entry.title: Tracking Settings for Books in SAP Enable Now - Is “Result” Possible?
entry.content:
Hello dear community,

My question concerns SCORM export and tracking for books. I’m familiar with the basics, but our client has a specific request:
They would like a book to be marked as “completed” in the tracking system once at least 80% of the pages have been viewed.
This requirement is new to me, as I’m only familiar with result tracking for quizzes or test-mode simulations, but I’d like to tap into the collective wisdom of the Enable Now experts.

Do any of you have experience with this or perhaps a workaround for this issue?
Is it possible to use the “Result as progress” tracking setting for this purpose?

Thank you very much,
Emelie

entry.title: Re: Mass Post Unplanned Asset Depreciation Postings
entry.content:
Hi,

Unfortunately, there is no update with respect to CE2608. there will be new API in AA but none of that allowed the posting of unplanned depreciation. However, that remains a roadmap topic.

BR,  MICK.

entry.title: Re: Issues facing during certification exam attempts and...
entry.content: @Margit_Wagner Still update system failure error popup coming while logging to SAP Learning HUB

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule Document Grounding: What I Learned the Hard Way
entry.content:
Hi Patty,

With the destination auth type OAuth2ClientCredentials in place of OAuth2Password (This requires includePaths to be specified in the pipeline every time a new folder is created in the site) we can avoid this problem and give the Sharepoint site admin more flexibility to Add and remove users read access to particular folders in the Site. Like this Joule will be able to fetch only those docs relevant to the user and not the entire site wide access.

entry.title: Re: Restart Failed E-Signatures Without Restarting the Entire Onboarding Process post 1H 2026 Releas
entry.content:
Hello @S0027664135 - This feature is in Prod. Besides the permission you mentioned, the email template "(ONB) Onboarding Task Restart Template" also needs to be set up to notify the new hire that this task has been restarted. 

After granting the permission, from the new hire in the Onboarding Dashboard, click on 'View Details' for the 'Complete e-Signature' task. Here, there is a button to 'Restart.' After clicking on it, there is a pop up window for an optional 'Reason for restart' (I suggest to skip this as it is visible for the new hire, unless you wish them to see it). This triggers the email to the new hire to resign the documents. This restart process can be done multiple times but cannot be undone.

If you cannot see the above, I'd suggest opening a ticket with SAP Support if you have not yet. This is all working in my Prod instance, in DC68.

~Julie

entry.title: ACADEMIC LESSONS: Your roadmap to teaching with SAP Learning Hub, student edition has new home
entry.content:
Our repository of academic lessons covering a range of educational topics and SAP solutions (with many featuring AI) has moved!  Please bookmark the new page and explore all the content and resources we offer for lecturers around the world!

Academic Lessons now have their own topic page along side all of the great content we offer for educators.

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/university-alliances/academic-lessons

This academic starter content continues to compliment the premium content available with Learning Hub, student edition that includes:  learning journeys, practical exercises featuring the latest SAP technologies, live sessions with experts, and annual certification attempts available to lecturers and students around the world at no cost.  

Each academic lesson includes: learning outcomes, description of the lesson, links to featured learning journeys, suggested student activities, review questions for discussion / group work / homework, lecture notes, teaching approaches, links to helpful resources, and suggested student deliverables.  Content supports business, technology and analytics classes. 

(Original Text from Heather)

We have asset acquired in FY26 and trying to change depreciation start date and getting error. we have no posting in FY2025 for the asse.

entry.title: MRP compensation strategy not working
entry.content:
The requirement is to run MRP in such a way that the independent requirement (VSFB) is 'offset' by the dependent requirements (OrdRes).

Even though we are using MRP strategy 70 and consumption indicator for planning requirements= 2, the MRP is not performing the compensation!

In the example below, the system should not have generated the planned order 5.400 kg

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule Document Grounding: What I Learned the Hard Way
entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you for the comment, this is very helpful. Just to make sure I understand correctly: would this approach also address the current issue where users receive the same answers from Joule even when they do not have access to the documents? My understanding is that, with folder-level permissions managed through SharePoint and the OAuth2ClientCredentials approach, Joule would only be able to retrieve documents that the specific user is authorized to access. Is that correct? I am aware that SAP is working on this topic and on improving document-level access control in Joule. However, as far as I know, this capability is not yet enabled across all regions and providers. Enable Document Access Control for Microsoft SharePoint | SAP Help Portal

Thank you in advance for your feedback.

entry.title: SAP on IBM i - Special considerations before upgrading the SAP Host Agent
entry.content:
In September 2025, SAP had released a security bulletin [CVE-2025-42958] for the operating system IBM i in SAP Note 3627373. It delivered patches for all supported SAP kernel releases on IBM i (7.22, 7.53, and 7.54). A month later, SAP Host Agent 7.22, patch level 69 was released. It contained code to overcome the vulnerability also for SAP systems that did not have the patches of SAP Note 3627373 applied. When the SAP Host Agent is applied in patch level 69 or higher and an SAP kernel does not have the patch level of SAP Note 3627373, there are cases when <sid>ADM user profiles are made unusable, so that the related SAP system can no longer be started.

So, before you upgrade the SAP Host Agent to the current level, make sure that your SAP systems all use the kernel patch levels that are documented in SAP Note 3627373. If you cannot apply a stack kernel (SAPEXE.SAR) in the specified level or higher, be aware that you need to apply the ILE patch for the fix, not disp+work. If you follow this rule, you are done and should not see any problems with <sid>ADM. If you want to know more about the background, you can read on.

What is the background?

Prior to security bulletin [CVE-2025-42958], all <sid>ADM user profiles were owned by group profile R3GROUP. In addition to the R3GROUP authorities that came from the ownership, additional private authorities were granted for the <sid>ADM user profile itself, so that it could be used for signing on and for database connections. Sharing the ownership of all <sid>ADM user profiles through R3GROUP was considered unsafe, so the ILE patches of SAP Note 3627373 changed the ownership of <sid>ADM to itself when the patch was applied and the SAP system was started. With that change, the private authority for <sid>ADM was no longer needed and dropped, and R3GROUP has no longer authority to <sid>ADM. So far, no issue, everything is running fine. These changes were implemented in the CRTSAPUSR tool of the kernel.

With SAP Host Agent patch level 69, code was introduced to scan the whole system for user profiles that are still owned by R3GROUP. If their naming followed the pattern <sid>ADM or <sid>OFR, the ownership was changed to itself. This code is contained in program R3SAP400/SAPINIT and executed when the SAP Host Agent is installed or upgraded, or when the system is IPLed.

With those changes in place, all <sid>ADM user profiles should be owned by itself. However, if you still have a kernel at a patch level prior to SAP Note 3627373, and if you have SAPCPE activated (see SAP Note 1632754), an old version of CRTSAPUSR may be executed by process SAPILEDX that reverts the ownership of <sid>ADM from itself to R3GROUP. During this process, the private authority for <sid>ADM is not granted, so that further attempts to use the <sid>ADM are failing.

If you experience that problem and cannot upgrade to the required ILE patch level immediately, you can solve the problem temporarily by changing the ownership of <sid>ADM back to itself. Unfortunately, each time the kernel library SAP<sid>IND is touched (e.g. during a backup), SAPILEDX will be executed and the ownership would be changed back to the incorrect value R3GROUP. To prevent this from happening, you can create a data area named SUPPORT in the kernel library as described in SAP Note 1637588. The data area should be deleted automatically when you apply the required ILE patch through the APYSIDKRN tool, but to be on the safe side, you should verify this and delete the data area manually if required.

This is the command to change the owner back to <sid>ADM:

CHGOBJOWN OBJ(<sid>ADM) OBJTYPE(*USRPRF) NEWOWN(<sid>ADM) CUROWNAUT(*REVOKE)

This is the command to create the data area SUPPORT in order to avoid changing the ownership back by SAPILEDX:

CRTDTAARA DTAARA(SAP<sid>IND/SUPPORT) TYPE(*CHAR) LEN(1)

Please note that the above is just a workaround. The recommended solution is to apply the ILE patches of SAP Note 3627373 for all affected SAP systems.

entry.title: Re: User Filtering for Repository Synchronization in SAP Identity Access Governance
entry.content:
Raised an influence request to enhance user filter to include 'inclusion' option as well in filter. This will be handy in test system when we want to sync limited users for testing purpose or to do POC.

Kindly vote: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/377340

entry.title: SAP TM + HSS Integration: Implementing Matrix Routing v2
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

If you are working with SAP Transportation Management (TM) and SAP HANA Spatial Services (HSS), you’ve likely seen the recent news regarding the Matrix Routing API. According to SAP Note 3648907, the Matrix Routing v1 API has reached its end-of-life (EOL) and must be migrated to v2 before September 2026.

Additionally, this migration includes a change in the licensing provider within the OEM model: the service is shifting from HERE to TomTom.

 

To migrate from Matrix Routing v1 API to Matrix Routing v2 API, you need to implement sap note 3695260. Follow regular steps you would on implementing any SAP Note, like checking pre-requisites.

 

Please be aware that evaluating your licensing models and parameters adjustments are be necessaire steps for this migration. Notes 3648907 and 3648910 can help you on that.

 

More information about HSS and new licensing model and parameters can be found in

Frequently Asked Questions for SAP HANA Spatial Services | SAP Help Portal

SAP HANA Spatial Services Reference  (link to 2025-10-30 version)

entry.title: Re: SAP TM + HSS Integration: Implementing Matrix Routing v2
entry.content: Thanks for sharing !

entry.title: Re: Issue with oauth authentication while calling Integration flow
entry.content:
Hi @Kunal_EFL,

just to clarify your question for me, you want to call a HTTPS endpoint in your IFlow and authenticate via OAuth? How is the HTTPS adapter in your IFlow configured? Is the flag "CSRF Protected" set?

Kind regards, Mathias
